name: Brainrot AI Meme
description: Chaotic fast-paced AI video, 9:16, 30-60s (optimal ~45s). Split-screen Remotion composition: top half is the AI-driven narrative (history fact, finance trivia, psychology, true crime, conspiracy, hot take) carried by AI voiceover and either a static AI image or short kling-v3.0-pro b-roll; bottom half is a hypnotic gameplay loop (Subway Surfers / Minecraft Parkour / GTA / Rocket League) supplied by the user. Big animated word-by-word captions burned across both halves carry the read. The dominant AI-generation format of 2026 — pattern-interrupts the scroll, generates 'what am I watching' comments, and scales cheaply per video. Reference-required: a gameplay-loop clip must be supplied or sanctioned by the user; AI-improvised gameplay reads as fake.

stackSummary.voiceover: ElevenLabs eleven_multilingual_v2 for the canonical narrator-grade read; eleven_turbo_v2_5 for fast/cheap iteration when generating dozens of variants. Voice picks: Adam, Onyx, or any deep-male narrator preset. Stability ~0.3, similarity boost ~0.75, style 0.4, energy/speaker_boost on. One TTS call per video, ~45s of audio.
stackSummary.topHalf: Either a single static AI image (google/gemini-3-pro-image-preview via OpenRouter) held for the full duration with subtle Remotion Ken Burns; OR 2-3 short kling-v3.0-pro b-roll clips (3-5s each, generate_audio: false) that loosely illustrate the narration; OR a Veo-style narration shot for niches that need a face. Top half = 50% of the 1080×1920 frame (1080×960).
stackSummary.bottomHalf: User-supplied gameplay loop (Subway Surfers, Minecraft Parkour, GTA, Rocket League). Loaded as a Remotion <Video> with native audio at low gain (~0.08) so SFX is felt but doesn't fight VO. Bottom half = remaining 50% (1080×960). Source: royalty-free / CC0 gameplay packs, or the user's own captured footage.
stackSummary.captions: ElevenLabs Scribe v1 for word-level timestamps on the VO track. Burned via existing src/lib/components/captions/HormoziCaptions.tsx (yellow Hormozi default) or a 'red-screaming-shake' variant for the most chaotic niches (true crime, conspiracy). Captions sit center-screen on the seam between the two halves, full-bleed across both, large weight, animated per-word.
stackSummary.music: Typically OFF. The gameplay diegetic SFX plus AI VO carry. If music is requested, ElevenLabs Music tense/curious bed at -18 dB under the VO.
stackSummary.composition: Single Remotion composition, 1080×1920 @ 30fps, ~45s. Layout: top half (AI visual), bottom half (gameplay loop), full-bleed caption layer, small 'AI-generated' C2PA disclosure overlay top-right (mandatory in 2026).

constants.structure: 0-2s SCREAMING hook → 2-40s narration over looping gameplay → 40-45s payoff/CTA

variationAxes: gameplay choice (Subway Surfers / Minecraft Parkour / GTA / Rocket League / satisfying soap-cutting / kinetic-sand)
VO tone (Reddit-narrator monotone / dramatic narrator / sarcastic / conspiratorial whisper)
niche (history fact / finance trivia / psychology / true crime / conspiracy / education-disguised / hot take)
top-half visual (single static AI image / 2-3 kling b-roll / talking-head AI avatar)
doNotCopyLiterally: VO script, exact gameplay clip, channel name, niche framing. Vibe and structural reference only. Always swap the gameplay source to one the user owns or has rights to.
referenceNotes: This format depends on a gameplay-loop background. The user can supply their own clip (Subway Surfers / Minecraft Parkour / GTA / Rocket League / similar hypnotic gameplay), OR fall back to the default CS:GO surf loop that auto-pulls from the ralphy-assets companion repo (see assets.csSurfLoop below). Without either, /ralph-art-director refuses (AGENTS.md hard rule #3) — AI-improvised gameplay reads as obviously fake and kills the format. If the brief names a specific real person, brand, or copyrighted IP for the top-half narrative, a reference for that subject is also required.

csSurfLoop.note: Default CS:GO surf gameplay loop — 1080x960, neon-ramps, hypnotic forward motion. Auto-pulled from the ralphy-assets companion repo at scaffold time and copied to assets/uploaded/gameplay-loop.mp4. Satisfies the reference-required gate when the user does not supply their own gameplay clip. Override by dropping a different file at the same path before render.
